steps.title: Fit the shown line first
steps.content: The graph shows $y=f(x)+14$. Two clear points from the line are $(0,2)$ and $(4,1)$. The slope is $$m=\frac{1-2}{4-0}=-\frac{1}{4},$$ and the $y$-intercept is $2$, so the graphed line is $y=-\frac{1}{4}x+2$.

steps.title: Undo the vertical shift
steps.content: Since the graphed function is $f(x)+14$, subtract 14 from the fitted equation: $$f(x)=-\frac{1}{4}x+2-14=-\frac{1}{4}x-12$$ So $f(x)$ must have y-intercept $-12$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A gives $f(x)=-\frac{1}{4}x-12$. Adding 14 gives $y=-\frac{1}{4}x+2$, exactly the graphed line. The other choices shift to y-intercepts $30$, $16$, or $0$, so they do not match the graph.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: $f(x)=-\frac{1}{4}x-12$ matches choice A. Choice A is correct.
